concept: each data point is a glass tube that fills with the subject's own photo to its value, side by side
code:
for i in range(n):
    frac = clamp(values[i]/vmax) * reveal
    x = RX0 + 20 + i*300
    w = 220; h = 600; y = 500
    img = images.get(labels[i]) if labels[i] in images else None
    fill_image(img, frac, x, y, w, h, direction='up', color=ACCENT)
    d.rounded_rectangle([x,y,x+w,y+h], outline=rgba(TEXT,255), width=4)
    text(labels[i], x+w/2, y+h+16, size=34, center=True)
    text(str(int(values[i])), x+w/2, y-60, size=48, color=HIGHLIGHT, center=True)

concept: two subjects face off, each emitting expanding sound rings whose count and reach scale with its decibel value
code:
cy = 600
wimg = images.get(labels[0])
jimg = images.get(labels[1])
for i in range(2):
    v = values[i]
    cxs = RX0 + 240 if i == 0 else RX1 - 240
    col = HIGHLIGHT if i == 0 else ACCENT
    rings = int(clamp(v / vmax) * 6.0 * reveal) + 1
    for k in range(rings):
        rr = 60 + k * 52
        a = int(190.0 * (1.0 - k / (rings + 1.0)) * reveal)
        d.ellipse([cxs - rr, cy - rr, cxs + rr, cy + rr],
                  outline=rgba(col, a), width=6)
img = wimg
for i in range(2):
    img = wimg if i == 0 else jimg
    cxs = RX0 + 240 if i == 0 else RX1 - 240
    if img is not None:
        asp = img.width / img.height
        w = 330
        h = int(w / asp)
        if h > 300:
            h = 300
            w = int(h * asp)
        paste(img, cxs - w // 2, cy - h // 2, w, h)
    col = HIGHLIGHT if i == 0 else ACCENT
    na = clamp((reveal - 0.4) / 0.6)
    text(str(int(values[i])) + ' dB', cxs, cy - 330, size=70, color=col, center=True)
    text(labels[i], cxs, cy + 210, size=40, center=True)
mid = clamp((reveal - 0.7) / 0.3)
if mid > 0:
    text('1.5x LOUDER', (RX0 + RX1) // 2, 950, size=54, color=WARN, center=True)
topic: Loudest Animals — whale vs jet engine

concept: the single subject sits at the center of sound rings radiating to the frame edges, its value huge at the top
code:
cx = (RX0 + RX1) // 2
cy = 620
img = images.get(labels[0])
rings = int(9.0 * reveal) + 1
for k in range(rings):
    rr = 100 + k * 95
    a = int(170.0 * (1.0 - k / (rings + 2.0)))
    d.ellipse([cx - rr, cy - rr, cx + rr, cy + rr],
              outline=rgba(HIGHLIGHT, a), width=8)
if img is not None:
    asp = img.width / img.height
    w = 660
    h = int(w / asp)
    if h > 500:
        h = 500
        w = int(h * asp)
    paste(img, cx - w // 2, cy - h // 2, w, h)
na = clamp((reveal - 0.3) / 0.5)
text(str(int(values[0])) + ' dB', cx, RTOP + 40, size=120,
     color=HIGHLIGHT, center=True)
text('the loudest animal EVER', cx, cy + 300, size=46, center=True)
text(labels[0], cx, cy + 360, size=40, color=ACCENT, center=True)

concept: two pressure gauges sweep to each contender's PSI, their photos mounted under the dials
code:
for i in range(2):
    v = values[i]
    cxs = RX0 + 250 if i == 0 else RX1 - 250
    cy = 560
    r = 210
    col = HIGHLIGHT if i == 0 else ACCENT
    d.arc([cxs - r, cy - r, cxs + r, cy + r], 150, 390,
          fill=rgba(TEXT, 90), width=16)
    sweep = 150.0 + 240.0 * clamp(v / vmax) * reveal
    d.arc([cxs - r, cy - r, cxs + r, cy + r], 150, int(sweep),
          fill=rgba(col, 255), width=16)
    ang = sweep * math.pi / 180.0
    nx = cxs + int((r - 30) * math.cos(ang))
    ny = cy + int((r - 30) * math.sin(ang))
    d.line([cxs, cy, nx, ny], fill=rgba(col, 255), width=10)
    d.ellipse([cxs - 16, cy - 16, cxs + 16, cy + 16], fill=rgba(col, 255))
    if i == 0:
        img = subject_image('saltwater crocodile open jaws close up')
    else:
        img = subject_image('human man smiling portrait face')
    if img is not None:
        asp = img.width / img.height
        w = 310
        h = int(w / asp)
        if h > 220:
            h = 220
            w = int(h * asp)
        paste(img, cxs - w // 2, cy + r + 10, w, h)
    text(str(int(v)) + ' PSI', cxs, cy - r - 100, size=62, color=col, center=True)
    text(labels[i], cxs, cy + r + 240, size=38, center=True)
mid = clamp((reveal - 0.7) / 0.3)
if mid > 0:
    text('23x STRONGER', (RX0 + RX1) // 2, RBOT - 80, size=56,
         color=WARN, center=True)
topic: two contenders on sweeping pressure dials

concept: a stylized world map with migration paths tracing the routes of each bird species, their images and values displayed at the terminus of each path
code:
for i in range(n):
    v = values[i]
    label = labels[i]
    img = subject_image(label)
    cx = RX0 + 200 + i * 260
    cy = RBOT - 200
    if img is not None:
        asp = img.width / img.height
        w = 140
        h = int(w / asp)
        paste(img, cx - w // 2, cy - h, w, h)
    path_x = [cx, cx, RX0 + 100, RX1 - 100, cx]
    path_y = [cy, RTOP + 100, RTOP + 100, RTOP + 100, cy]
    for j in range(len(path_x) - 1):
        dx = path_x[j + 1] - path_x[j]
        dy = path_y[j + 1] - path_y[j]
        dist = math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy)
        dx /= dist
        dy /= dist
        steps = int(dist * reveal * 10)
        for k in range(steps):
            x = path_x[j] + k * dx * dist / steps
            y = path_y[j] + k * dy * dist / steps
            d.ellipse([x - 2, y - 2, x + 2, y + 2], fill=rgba(ACCENT, 255))
    text(str(int(v)) + ' miles', cx, cy + 20, size=36, color=TEXT, center=True)
    text(label, cx, cy - 40, size=32, center=True)

concept: a cargo crane lifts shipping containers filled to each ship's cargo capacity, with the ship's photo and label displayed alongside
code:
for i in range(n):
    v = values[i]
    cxs = RX0 + 250 if i == 0 else RX1 - 250
    cy = RTOP + 200
    r = 80
    col = HIGHLIGHT if i == 0 else ACCENT
    d.ellipse([cxs - r, cy - r, cxs + r, cy + r], fill=rgba(TEXT, 90))
    fill_image(subject_image('shipping container'), clamp(v / vmax) * reveal, cxs - r, cy - r, r * 2, r * 2)
    img = subject_image(labels[i])
    if img is not None:
        asp = img.width / img.height
        w = 200
        h = int(w / asp)
        paste(img, cxs - w // 2, cy + r + 20, w, h)
    text(str(int(v)) + ' TEU', cxs, cy + r + 150, size=46, color=col, center=True)
    text(labels[i], cxs, cy + r + 220, size=38, center=True)
    crane_y = lerp(cy + r + 20, cy + r + 150, reveal)
    d.line([cxs, crane_y, cxs, cy + r + 150], fill=rgba(col, 255), width=8)

concept: two ancient scrolls with images of food unroll to reveal the year and recipe name, the length of the unrolled portion representing the time period
code:
for i in range(n):
    v = values[i]
    cxs = RX0 + 250 if i == 0 else RX1 - 250
    cy = RTOP + 200
    img = subject_image('ancient scroll')
    if img is not None:
        asp = img.width / img.height
        w = 200
        h = int(w / asp)
        paste(img, cxs - w // 2, cy - h // 2, w, h)
    scroll_w = 20
    scroll_h = 600 * clamp(v / vmax) * reveal
    d.rectangle([cxs - scroll_w // 2, cy + h // 2, cxs + scroll_w // 2, cy + h // 2 + scroll_h], fill=rgba(ACCENT, 200))
    text(labels[i], cxs, cy + h // 2 + scroll_h + 20, size=38, color=TEXT, center=True)
    text(str(int(v)) + ' years ago', cxs, cy + h // 2 + scroll_h + 60, size=38, color=TEXT, center=True)
    recipe_img = subject_image('recipe book page')
    if recipe_img is not None:
        asp = recipe_img.width / recipe_img.height
        rw = 150
        rh = int(rw / asp)
        paste(recipe_img, cxs - rw // 2, cy + h // 2 + scroll_h + 100, rw, rh)

concept: a set of shelves where each data point is represented by a jar filled with its corresponding ingredient, with the jar size and filling level indicating its value
code:
cx = (RX0 + RX1) // 2
cy = RTOP + 200
n = len(values)
shelf_h = 120
shelf_gap = 40
for i in range(n):
    v = values[i]
    label = labels[i]
    img = subject_image(label)
    if img is not None:
        asp = img.width / img.height
        w = 80
        h = int(w / asp)
        if h > 100:
            h = 100
            w = int(h * asp)
        shelf_y = cy + i * (shelf_h + shelf_gap)
        jar_w = 100
        jar_h = 150
        fill_h = int(jar_h * clamp(v / vmax) * reveal)
        d.rectangle([cx - jar_w // 2, shelf_y, cx + jar_w // 2, shelf_y + shelf_h], fill=rgba(ACCENT, 10))
        d.rectangle([cx - jar_w // 2, shelf_y + shelf_h - fill_h, cx + jar_w // 2, shelf_y + shelf_h], fill=rgba(HIGHLIGHT, 255))
        paste(img, cx - w // 2, shelf_y + shelf_h + 10, w, h)
        text(str(int(v)), cx, shelf_y + shelf_h + 120, size=36, color=TEXT, center=True)
        text(label, cx, shelf_y - 30, size=30, color=TEXT, center=True)

concept: a microscope zooms in on a subject image, with a scale bar and labels showing the data points
code:
# get subject images
hair_img = subject_image('human hair')
graphene_img = subject_image('graphene sheet')

# zoom level based on reveal progress
zoom = lerp(1, 100, reveal)

# center of the screen
cx = (RX0 + RX1) // 2
cy = (RTOP + RBOT) // 2

# draw microscope view
d.rectangle([cx - 300, cy - 300, cx + 300, cy + 300], fill=rgba((0, 0, 0), 150))

# draw subject images
if hair_img is not None:
    asp = hair_img.width / hair_img.height
    w = 200
    h = int(w / asp)
    paste(hair_img, cx - 250, cy - 150, w, h)
if graphene_img is not None:
    asp = graphene_img.width / graphene_img.height
    w = 50
    h = int(w / asp)
    paste(graphene_img, cx + 100, cy - 25, w, h)

# draw scale bar
d.line([cx - 200, cy + 200, cx + 200, cy + 200], fill=rgba(TEXT, 255), width=5)
text(str(int(values[0])) + ' nm', cx - 200, cy + 220, size=30, color=TEXT, center=False)
text(str(int(values[1])) + ' nm', cx + 180, cy + 220, size=30, color=TEXT, center=False)

# draw labels
text(labels[0], cx - 250, cy - 250, size=30, color=TEXT, center=False)
text(labels[1], cx + 100, cy - 50, size=30, color=TEXT, center=False)

# zoom text
text('Zoom: ' + str(int(zoom)) + 'x', cx, cy + 280, size=30, color=TEXT, center=True)

concept: a nano-membrane model is built from hexagonal cells filled with subject images, with each cell's size and fill amount representing the corresponding data point's value and label
code:
cx = (RX0 + RX1) // 2
cy = (RTOP + RBOT) // 2
cell_size = 80
n = len(values)
for i in range(n):
    v = values[i]
    x = cx + (i - n // 2) * (cell_size * 1.5)
    y = cy
    d.ellipse([x - cell_size // 2, y - cell_size // 2, x + cell_size // 2, y + cell_size // 2],
               outline=rgba(HIGHLIGHT, 150), width=4)
    fill_frac = clamp(v / vmax) * reveal
    fill_image(subject_image('nano-particles'), fill_frac, x - cell_size // 2, y - cell_size // 2, cell_size, cell_size, 'up', rgba(ACCENT, 150))
    text(str(int(v)) + ' nm', x, y + cell_size // 2 + 20, size=32, color=TEXT, center=True)
    text(labels[i], x, y - cell_size // 2 - 30, size=28, color=TEXT, center=True)

concept: a scale with two plates, one for companies investing in thin materials and the other for companies using them, each plate's weight and label rising as the reveal progresses
code:
cx = (RX0 + RX1) // 2
cy = (RTOP + RBOT) // 2
d.line([cx, RTOP, cx, RBOT], fill=rgba(TEXT, 90), width=4)
for i in range(n):
    v = values[i]
    label = labels[i]
    if i == 0:
        px = cx - 200
    else:
        px = cx + 200
    py = cy + (v / vmax) * (RBOT - cy) * reveal
    d.ellipse([px - 20, py - 20, px + 20, py + 20], fill=rgba(ACCENT if i == 0 else HIGHLIGHT, 255))
    img = subject_image('microchip' if i == 0 else 'solar panel')
    if img is not None:
        asp = img.width / img.height
        w = 120
        h = int(w / asp)
        paste(img, px - w // 2, py + 30, w, h)
    text(label, px, py + 160, size=36, center=True)
    text(str(int(v)) + '%', px, py - 100, size=48, center=True)

concept: Each magnetic material is represented by a colored orb that rises and fills with a photo of the material as its ranking value increases
code:
for i in range(n):
    v = values[i]
    cxs = RX0 + 200 + i * 300
    cy = RBOT - 100
    r = 120
    col = HIGHLIGHT if i == 0 else ACCENT
    d.ellipse([cxs - r, cy - r, cxs + r, cy + r], fill=rgba(TEXT, 90))
    fill_frac = clamp(v / vmax) * reveal
    fill_image(subject_image(labels[i]), fill_frac, cxs - r, cy - r, r * 2, r * 2, 'up', col)
    d.ellipse([cxs - r, cy - r, cxs + r, cy + r], outline=rgba(col, 255), width=8)
    text(str(int(v)) + ' units', cxs, cy - r - 80, size=48, color=col, center=True)
    text(labels[i], cxs, cy + r + 20, size=32, color=TEXT, center=True)
    if reveal > 0.5:
        rise = lerp(RBOT, RTOP + 200, reveal * 0.5)
        d.line([cxs, rise, cxs, cy], fill=rgba(col, 128), width=4)
